texto: And every science, when we understand it not as an instrument of power and domination but as an adventure in knowledge pursued by our species across the ages, is nothing but this harmony, more or less vast, more or less rich from one epoch to another, which unfurls over the course of generations and centuries, by the delicate counterpoint of all the themes appearing in turn, as if summoned from the void.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alexandre Grothendieck: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “And every science, when we understand it not as an instrument of power and domination but as an a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, science, every pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
